text: A teaspoon of a neutron star weighs about 6 billion tons.
Hot water freezes faster than cold water — this is called the Mpemba effect.
Lightning strikes the Earth about 8 million times per day.
Bananas are naturally radioactive due to their potassium content.
Glass is technically neither a solid nor a liquid — it's an amorphous solid.
Sound travels about 4.3 times faster in water than in air.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast 100,000 slices of bread.
The human brain generates about 20 watts of electrical power.
If you could fold a piece of paper 42 times, it would reach the moon.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
Honey never spoils — edible 3000-year-old honey was found in Egyptian tombs.
Water can boil and freeze at the same time — it's called the triple point.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
Atoms are 99.9999999% empty space.
The smell of rain has a name — it's called petrichor.
DNA in a single human cell stretches to about 6 feet when uncoiled.
Diamonds can be made from peanut butter under extreme pressure.
Mars sunsets appear blue because of the way dust particles scatter light.
A photon takes 8 minutes to reach Earth from the Sun but 100,000 years to travel from the Sun's core to its surface.
The coldest temperature ever recorded in a lab is 0.0000000001 Kelvin.
Helium was discovered on the Sun before it was found on Earth.
A tablespoon of a neutron star material weighs about 1 billion tons.
Electrons can exist in two places at the same time — quantum superposition.
The human genome contains about 3 billion base pairs of DNA.
Gravity travels at the speed of light.
There are more bacteria in your mouth than there are people on Earth.
If the Sun were the size of a front door, Earth would be the size of a nickel.
Static electricity can reach voltages of 25,000 volts.
Water expands by about 9% when it freezes into ice.
A single human cell contains about 6 feet of DNA when uncoiled.
The speed of light in a vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 meters per second.
Black holes can evaporate over time through Hawking radiation.
Absolute zero is −273.15°C — the lowest possible temperature.
A googol is the number 1 followed by 100 zeros.
The Earth's core is as hot as the surface of the Sun — about 5,500°C.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum — there are no particles to vibrate.
Lasers can be cooled to temperatures colder than outer space.
The strongest known material is graphene — 200 times stronger than steel.
Time moves slightly faster at the top of a building than at the bottom.
Every atom in your body is billions of years old.
Quantum entanglement allows particles to communicate instantaneously across any distance.
The Higgs boson was predicted in 1964 but not discovered until 2012.
A single lightning bolt can heat the air around it to 30,000 Kelvin.
The Fibonacci sequence appears naturally in sunflowers, pinecones, and shells.
Supercooled water can remain liquid below 0°C until disturbed.
Rubber bands last longer when refrigerated.
The human body emits a small amount of visible light — too faint for eyes to see.
A piece of paper cannot be folded more than about 7 times by hand.
Quantum computers can solve certain problems millions of times faster than classical ones.
The color orange was named after the fruit, not the other way around.
Sunlight takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.
A single human hair can support up to 100 grams of weight.
There are more ways to arrange a deck of cards than atoms on Earth.
Mercury and Venus are the only planets with no moons.
The speed of sound is about 767 mph at sea level.
Liquid nitrogen boils at −196°C.
Antimatter is the most expensive substance — about $62.5 trillion per gram.
A cubic inch of bone can bear a load of 19,000 pounds.
The Earth receives about 430 quintillion joules of solar energy per hour.
Graphene is so thin that 3 million sheets stacked would be just 1mm thick.
Plasma is the most common state of matter in the universe, not solid.
The pH of pure water changes with temperature.
Radio waves travel at the speed of light.
A teaspoon of water contains about 1.67 sextillion molecules.
The Moon is slowly drifting away from Earth at 3.8 cm per year.
Superconductors have zero electrical resistance below critical temperature.
The Doppler effect works for both sound and light.

Dark matter makes up about 27% of the universe.
The double-slit experiment proves light behaves as both wave and particle.
Piezoelectric crystals generate electricity when squeezed.
The half-life of carbon-14 is approximately 5,730 years.
Ferrofluid is a liquid that becomes magnetized in the presence of a magnetic field.
Maxwell's equations unified electricity and magnetism in 1865.
Photosynthesis converts about 1-2% of sunlight into chemical energy.
Neutrinos can pass through an entire planet without hitting a single atom.
The loudest sound on Earth was the eruption of Krakatoa in 1883 — heard 3,000 miles away.
Entropy always increases in a closed system — the second law of thermodynamics.
The Casimir effect proves that empty space isn't truly empty.
Fluorescent lights work by exciting mercury vapor to produce ultraviolet light.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
Buckminsterfullerene (C60) is shaped like a soccer ball at the molecular level.
Quantum tunneling allows particles to pass through barriers they shouldn't be able to.
Human-made elements above 118 on the periodic table are theoretically possible.
Metamaterials can bend light around objects, potentially enabling invisibility.
The observable universe contains about 2 trillion galaxies.
Bose-Einstein condensate is a fifth state of matter created near absolute zero.
Penicillin was discovered by accident when mold contaminated a petri dish.
The uncertainty principle means you can never know both position and momentum exactly.
Volcanic lightning occurs when ash particles collide and generate static electricity.
Magnetars are neutron stars with magnetic fields a quadrillion times Earth's.
The photoelectric effect proved light can behave as particles — earning Einstein the Nobel.
Superfluids can flow uphill and through molecularly thin cracks.
There are 118 confirmed elements on the periodic table.
A googolplex is 10 raised to the power of a googol.
Cosmic rays from deep space constantly bombard Earth.

Graphene can conduct electricity better than copper.
The placebo effect works even when patients know they're taking a placebo.
Tidal forces from the Moon generate about 3.7 terawatts of energy.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the building of the Great Pyramid.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes — Britain vs Zanzibar in 1896.
Napoleon was actually above-average height for his time at 5'7".
Ancient Romans used crushed mouse brains as toothpaste.
The Great Fire of London in 1666 officially killed only 6 people.
Vikings used to give kittens to new brides as wedding gifts.

The oldest known recipe is for beer, dating back to 4000 BC.

The first alarm clock could only ring at 4 AM.

Turkeys were once worshipped as gods by the Mayan civilization.
The Eiffel Tower was originally intended to be a temporary structure.
Ancient Egyptians used slabs of stone as pillows.
Genghis Khan killed an estimated 40 million people — about 10% of the world's population.
The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace in 1843.
Carrots were originally purple, not orange.

Roman gladiators rarely fought to the death — they were too expensive to train.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye.

Alexander the Great named over 70 cities after himself — and one after his horse.

King Henry VIII had six wives — and he had two of them executed.

The Marvel character Deadpool is aware he's a comic book character.
The longest-running Broadway show is The Phantom of the Opera.
The world's most translated book after the Bible is The Little Prince.
League of Legends has over 100 million active monthly players.
The first emoticon was :-) used by Scott Fahlman in 1982.
Baby Shark is the most-viewed YouTube video ever with 14+ billion views.
The first arcade video game was Computer Space in 1971.
Twitch streamers have been watched for over 1 trillion minutes.
Friends was originally titled 'Insomnia Café'.
The PlayStation 2 is the best-selling console ever at 155 million units.
The original Star Wars trilogy was almost called 'Adventures of Luke Starkiller'.
The first podcast was created by Dave Winer and Adam Curry in 2004.
Wikipedia has over 60 million articles across 300+ languages.
The most expensive painting ever sold was 'Salvator Mundi' for $450 million.
The term 'going viral' became popular in the mid-2000s with YouTube.
SpongeBob SquarePants was created by a marine science educator.
The average Netflix user watches about 1.5 hours per day.
Minecraft has sold over 300 million copies worldwide.
The first reality TV show was 'An American Family' in 1973.
The world record for most Rubik's Cube solves in 24 hours is over 6,000.
LinkedIn was founded in 2002 — before Facebook, YouTube, and Twitter.
The phrase 'break the internet' became a meme after Kim Kardashian's Paper cover.
